yesterday.s: Wendy was pained too to find that the past year was but as yesterday to Peter; it had seemed such a long year of waiting to her.

yield.s: Sometimes the thing yielded to him without a contest, but there were occasions when it would have been better for the house if he had swallowed his pride and used a made-up tie.

yo.n: A more villainous-looking lot never hung in a row on Execution dock.
yo.s: "Avast belay, yo ho, heave to, A-pirating we go, And if we're parted by a shot We're sure to meet below!"

yo.n: At once the lost boys--but where are they?
yo.s: "Yo ho, yo ho, the pirate life, The flag o' skull and bones, A merry hour, a hempen rope, And hey for Davy Jones."

yo.n: To terrorize the prisoners the more, though with a certain loss of dignity, he danced along an imaginary plank, grimacing at them as he sang; and when he finished he cried, "Do you want a touch of the cat [o' nine tails] before you walk the plank?"
yo.s: "Yo ho, yo ho, the frisky plank, You walks along it so, Till it goes down and you goes down To Davy Jones below!"

yo.n: What was the last line will never be known, for of a sudden the song was stayed by a dreadful screech from the cabin.
yo.s: "Yo ho, yo ho, the scratching cat, Its tails are nine, you know, And when they're writ upon your back--"

yore.n: He gave his hat to Liza, who took it scornfully; for she had no imagination, and was quite incapable of understanding the motives of such a man.
yore.s: As Mr. Darling puts his head out to kiss his wife, we see that his face is more worn than of yore, but has a softer expression.

you.n: Without more words they fell to, and for a space there was no advantage to either blade.
you.s: "Dark and sinister man," Peter answered, "have at thee."

young.n: He really knew nothing about it, he had merely suspicions, but he said at a venture, "Wendy, I ran away the day I was born."
young.s: "I don't know," he replied uneasily, "but I am quite young."

young.n: So, though fear was upon her, and she longed to hear male voices, she would not waken them.
young.s: But she was a young mother and she did not know this; she thought you simply must stick to your rule about half an hour after the mid-day meal.

young.n: "Tink," he rapped out, "if you don't get up and dress at once I will open the curtains, and then we shall all see you in your negligee [nightgown]."
young.s: Nibs exclaimed, aghast at such insubordination, whereupon Peter went sternly toward the young lady's chamber.

young.n: It may have been Quixotic, but it was magnificent.
young.s: Inwardly he must have suffered torture; but he preserved a calm exterior even when the young criticised his little home, and he always lifted his hat courteously to any lady who looked inside.

young.n: "John," he said, looking around him doubtfully, "I think I have been here before."
young.s: They alighted on the floor, quite unashamed of themselves, and the youngest one had already forgotten his home.

young.n: It was then that Mrs. Darling began playing again.
young.s: "It is very careless of mother," said that young scoundrel John, "not to be here when we come back."

youngster.n: Great were the rejoicings when Peter reached the home under the ground almost as soon as Wendy, who had been carried hither and thither by the kite.
youngster.s: As we shall not see her again, it may be worth mentioning here that all Never birds now build in that shape of nest, with a broad brim on which the youngsters take an airing.

youth.n: "Dark and sinister man," Peter answered, "have at thee."
youth.s: "Proud and insolent youth," said Hook, "prepare to meet thy doom."

youth.n: This, of course, was nonsense; but it was proof to the unhappy Hook that Peter did not know in the least who or what he was, which is the very pinnacle of good form.
youth.s: "I'm youth, I'm joy," Peter answered at a venture, "I'm a little bird that has broken out of the egg."
